seo.title: AI Agent Authentication and Authorization in Instructor Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent authentication and authorization in Instructor agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Instructor-powered AI agents, authentication and authorization failures manifest as structured output validation errors when models fail to extract valid credentials, tokens, or permission scopes from dynamic API responses or user inputs. Agents using Instructor's Pydantic patching often encounter retries exhaustion or hallucinated enum values for OAuth scopes and JWT claims, breaking secure access flows. These issues cascade into unauthorized API calls or credential leakage in multi-step agent workflows.

howItManifests.scenario: OAuth token extraction from login response
howItManifests.impact: Agent stuck in infinite retry loop, unable to proceed to authorized API calls

howItManifests.scenario: Permission scope validation from RBAC response
howItManifests.symptom: Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
howItManifests.impact: Agent assumes unauthorized permissions, makes privilege escalation calls

howItManifests.scenario: JWT claims parsing from identity provider
howItManifests.impact: Agent operates with incomplete user context, violates least privilege

howItManifests.symptom: Nested object extraction producing inconsistent depth
howItManifests.impact: Agent misinterprets inherited permissions, exposes sensitive resources

detection.name: Retry Exhaustion Monitor
detection.description: Detects authentication flows stuck in Instructor's validation retry loop
detection.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

class AuthToken(BaseModel):
    access_token: str
    token_type: Literal['Bearer']
    expires_in: int

@contextmanager
def monitor_retries():
    start_time = time.time()
    retries = 0
    try:
        yield
    except Exception as e:
        retries = getattr(e, 'retries_exhausted', 0)
        if retries > 3 and 'access_token' in str(e):
            print(f'🚨 AUTH RETRY EXHAUSTED: {retries} attempts')
        raise

with monitor_retries():
    token = client.messages.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        response_model=AuthToken,
        messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Extract OAuth token: {"access_token": "xyz"}'}]
    )

detection.name: Enum Hallucination Detector
detection.description: Catches model generating invalid OAuth scope enums during permission extraction
detection.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, validator
from typing import Literal

class PermissionScope(BaseModel):
    scopes: List[Literal['read:users', 'write:users', 'admin:users']]

    @validator('scopes')
    def validate_scopes(cls, v):
        invalid_scopes = [s for s in v if s not in ['read:users', 'write:users', 'admin:users']]
        if invalid_scopes:
            print(f'🚨 HALLUCINATED SCOPES: {invalid_scopes}')
            raise ValueError(f'Invalid scopes: {invalid_scopes}')
        return v

# Usage
response = client.messages.create(
    model='gpt-4o',
    response_model=PermissionScope,
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Extract permissions from: read:users,write:docs'}]
)

detection.name: Missing Claims Audit
detection.description: Identifies partial JWT claim extraction missing critical authorization fields

fixes.name: Fallback Credential Extraction
fixes.description: Implements regex fallback + LLM extraction with partial success handling
fixes.code:
import re
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

class SafeAuthToken(BaseModel):
    access_token: str
    token_type: str = 'Bearer'
    expires_in: int = 3600

@patch
class SafeAuthTokenWithFallback(SafeAuthToken):
    @root_validator(pre=True)
    def extract_fallback(cls, values):
        text = values.get('content', '')
        # Fallback regex for common token patterns
        token_match = re.search(r'"access_token"\s*:\s*"([^"]+)"', text)
        if token_match:
            values['access_token'] = token_match.group(1)
        return values

# Usage
token = client.messages.create(
    model='gpt-4o',
    response_model=SafeAuthTokenWithFallback,
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Login response: {"access_token": "abc123"}'}]
)

fixes.name: Strict Enum Coercion
fixes.description: Maps hallucinated scopes to valid enums using semantic similarity

fixes.name: Nested Role Resolver
fixes.description: Recursively validates nested role hierarchies with depth limiting

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Authentication and Authorization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically detects Instructor authentication failures by monitoring `client.chat.completions.create()` calls wrapped with `vex.verify()`. It captures structured output validation errors, retry exhaustion patterns, and schema violations specific to credential extraction flows. Vex's agent traces reveal exactly which Pydantic models fail during auth flows and why.

Remediation is automatic: Vex applies context-aware retries with credential-specific prompts, injects regex fallbacks for token extraction, and validates enum coercion before production deployment. For nested role hierarchies, Vex enforces depth limits and generates synthetic test cases covering edge-case JWT claims and OAuth responses.

Integration takes 2 lines of code and catches 95% of auth-related agent failures before they impact production workflows.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex + Instructor today and eliminate auth failures in your AI agents with one wrapper.
